title: Northeastern University president’s compensation exceeds $3 million, records show
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Northeastern University's president Joseph Aoun received over $3 million in total compensation for Fiscal Year 2025.
Aoun's pay increased significantly from FY21 to FY22, jumping 113% due to deferred compensation and bonuses.
Aoun's earnings surpass MIT president Sally Kornbluth's by nearly $1 million, with Kornbluth earning $2,151,788 in FY25.

title: Cambridge man killed in early-morning shooting, investigators say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Xavier Bautista, 32, was found dead from a gunshot wound near Broadway and Norfolk Street in Cambridge.
Cambridge police received a 911 call around 5:30 a.m. reporting Bautista's body on the ground.
Cambridge police, Massachusetts State Police, and the DA’s office are actively investigating the early-morning shooting.

title: New England Aquarium trainer joins effort to save endangered penguin chicks in South Africa
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Diana Major from New England Aquarium helped care for endangered penguin chicks in Cape Town.
Major volunteered with the Southern African Foundation for the Conservation of Coastal Birds in April.
African penguins are critically endangered, facing threats like habitat loss, food scarcity, and disease.

title: Red Sox’ Willson Contreras apologizes for recent on-field confrontations: ‘It’s been a really tough week’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Willson Contreras is appealing a seven-game MLB suspension following a brawl with the Nationals' pitcher Cade Cavalli.
Contreras has been emotional, citing stress due to recent natural disasters in Venezuela impacting his performance and behavior.
Both Contreras and Cavalli have apologized for their actions during the game, acknowledging their competitive nature.

title: Red Sox Aroldis Chapman and Ranger Suarez selected to MLB All-Star Game
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Aroldis Chapman and Ranger Suarez will represent the Boston Red Sox in the 2026 MLB All-Star game.
Chapman boasts a 2.10 ERA with 33 strikeouts in 25.2 innings this season, along with 17 saves.
Suarez, returning to Philadelphia for the All-Star game, has a 2.94 ERA and 92 strikeouts in 88.2 innings.

title: Paul Pierce encourages Jaylen Brown after trade from Celtics: ‘This is what keeps you hungry’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Paul Pierce comments on Celtics trading Jaylen Brown to the 76ers, expressing disappointment but understanding the decision.
Pierce encourages Brown to use the perceived lack of respect as motivation to excel in his career.
Jaylen Brown cites feeling disrespected by the trade and his history with the Celtics' decisions.

title: Photos: Boston celebrates the Fourth of July
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Boston began its Fourth of July celebration with a parade and a reading of the Declaration of Independence.
Thousands gathered at the Esplanade for the 2026 Boston Pops Fireworks Spectacular, featuring drone shows and performances.
Prominent attendees included Mayor Michelle Wu and Massachusetts Governor Maura Healey during the July 4th festivities.

title: Is Caleb Durbin’s turnaround enough to solidify his future with the Red Sox?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Caleb Durbin leads the Red Sox in OPS with .977 and has 7 home runs in the last 30 days.
Durbin began the season poorly but improved significantly after May, hitting .321 with .945 OPS since May 28.
The Red Sox have team control over Durbin until 2031, offering flexibility for future roster decisions.

title: Red Sox’ Aroldis Chapman breaks MLB relief pitcher strikeout record in save vs. Angels
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Aroldis Chapman set a new MLB record for reliever strikeouts, reaching 1,364 in the Red Sox win over the Angels.
Chapman surpassed Hoyt Wilhelm, needing only 847 innings compared to Wilhelm's 1,872.1 innings to achieve the record.
Chapman, 38, is a potential trade candidate despite recent success, as the Red Sox face a 13% playoff chance.

title: Allstate names the most collision-prone cities in the U.S.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston is the most collision-prone U.S. city, with drivers involved in crashes every 3.7 years, per Allstate.
Worcester and Springfield, MA, rank 197th and 196th, respectively, among U.S. cities for collision frequency.
Allstate's report uses insurance claims and app data to assess driving habits linked to collision risks.
